quiz.loadingSubtitle: Analysing your answers with ancient wisdom

results.constitutionNote: This is your Prakriti — your constitutional nature since birth.
results.constitutionDetail: It reflects your innate tendencies, not your current state of imbalance (Vikriti). If you feel your current experience differs significantly from this profile, consider consulting a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
results.disclaimerDetail: This assessment is for educational and wellness purposes only. It is not a clinical diagnosis and does not replace consultation with a licensed Ayurvedic physician or healthcare provider.
results.morningDefault: Start your day with intention and grounding practices.
results.midDayDetail: Eat your largest meal now — digestion is strongest.
results.quoteTranslation: To maintain the health of the healthy
results.practitionerNote: A qualified Ayurvedic practitioner (Vaidya) can assess your Vikriti — your current state of imbalance — and give you a truly personalised protocol based on pulse diagnosis (Nadi Pariksha) and physical examination.
results.vsSubtitle: Your constitution vs. your current state of imbalance
results.inBalanceDetail: Your current state aligns with your constitution. Continue following your {type} recommendations.
results.aggravatedDetail: Temporarily add {type}-balancing practices to your routine. Consider consulting a Vaidya for a personalised dual-dosha protocol.
results.howFeeling: How are you feeling right now?
results.howFeelingDesc: Your Prakriti is your lifelong constitution. But your Vikriti — your current state of imbalance — can shift with seasons, stress, and lifestyle.
results.vikritiCta: Take the 5-question Vikriti assessment to discover which dosha is elevated right now and what to focus on immediately.
results.doshaDesc_Vata-Pitta: Air & Fire · Creative and driven · Light and intense
results.doshaDesc_Pitta-Kapha: Fire & Earth · Focused and steady · Determined and grounded
results.doshaDesc_Vata-Kapha: Air & Earth · Creative and caring · Light and nurturing
results.doshaDesc_Tridoshic: All five elements · Adaptable · Resilient · Balanced

about.heroDesc: Prakriti (Sanskrit: प्रकृति) means your innate nature — the unique combination of three bio-energies (doshas) that was established at conception and defines your mind-body constitution for life.
about.sciencePara1: Ayurveda (Sanskrit: आयुर्वेद) is one of the world’s oldest holistic healing systems, developed in India over 5,000 years ago. The word literally means the “science of life” (Ayur = life, Veda = science or knowledge).
about.sciencePara2: Unlike modern medicine which focuses on treating disease, Ayurveda focuses on maintaining health by understanding and working with your unique constitution. At its core is the belief that each person has a unique combination of energies that influences everything from digestion to personality.
about.sciencePara3: Your Prakriti — your individual constitution — is determined at birth and remains constant throughout your life. Understanding it is the foundation of Ayurvedic self-care.
about.doshasSubtitle: Every person is born with all three doshas, but in different proportions. The dominant dosha(s) define your Prakriti.
about.vataTagline: The force of movement and creativity
about.vataDesc: Vata governs all movement in the body — breathing, circulation, nerve impulses, and the movement of thoughts. When in balance, Vata types are creative, enthusiastic, and joyful. When out of balance, they can experience anxiety, insomnia, and digestive irregularities.
about.pittaTagline: The force of metabolism and transformation
about.pittaDesc: Pitta governs digestion, metabolism, and transformation. It controls how we process food, thoughts, and experiences. Balanced Pitta types are intelligent, decisive, and focused. Imbalanced Pitta manifests as anger, inflammation, and perfectionism.
about.kaphaTagline: The force of structure and nourishment
about.kaphaDesc: Kapha provides the physical structure and lubrication for the body. It governs growth, strength, and immunity. Balanced Kapha types are calm, loving, and strong. Out of balance, they can become lethargic, resistant to change, or prone to congestion.
about.ctaDesc: Take our 25-question quiz based on classical Ayurvedic texts and discover your unique Prakriti in under 5 minutes.
